weeks.title: Week 1 — CORS, Axios wiring + public API endpoints
weeks.description: Connect your React frontend to your live Spring Boot API. Get data flowing end-to-end.

days.label: Day 1 · Monday
days.title: Fix CORS on the Spring Boot side

tasks.text: Learn: what CORS is — browser blocks cross-origin requests unless server explicitly permits them
In SecurityFilterChain: configure CorsConfigurationSource bean
Set allowedOrigins to your Vercel URL (and http://localhost:5173 for dev)
Set allowedMethods: GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, OPTIONS
Set allowedHeaders: * and allowCredentials: true
Test: open browser DevTools → Network tab → confirm no CORS error on OPTIONS preflight

days.resource: Common mistake: Adding @CrossOrigin on the controller is not enough when using Spring Security — you must configure CORS inside SecurityFilterChain or it gets blocked by the security filter before reaching the controller.

days.label: Day 2 · Tuesday
days.title: Axios instance + environment variables

tasks.text: In React: create src/api/axiosInstance.js with baseURL from import.meta.env.VITE_API_URL
Set .env.local: VITE_API_URL=http://localhost:8080/api/v1
Add request interceptor: read token from AuthContext (or memory store), attach as Authorization: Bearer {token}
Add response interceptor: on 401 response, call logout() and redirect to /login
Update all src/api/*.js files to use axiosInstance instead of fetch
Test: call GET /api/v1/products from React — confirm real data from MySQL appears

days.resource: Token access tip: Axios interceptors run outside React components, so you can't call useContext() inside them. Store the token in a plain JS module variable (a "token store") that AuthContext writes to on login — the interceptor reads from there.

tasks.text: Update useProducts() hook: queryFn calls real GET /api/v1/products?page=&size=
Update useProduct(id) hook: queryFn calls real GET /api/v1/products/{id}
Pass page and size as query params — driven by pagination state in ProductsPage
Map Spring Boot's Page response object (content, totalPages, totalElements) to the UI
Test: browse products on the React app — paginate, click through to detail page
Wire search: GET /api/v1/products/search?name= — update useProducts to accept a search param
Build useCategories() hook — fetches GET /api/v1/categories
Add category filter to ProductsPage — dropdown populated from real categories API
On category select: add categoryId param to products query
Add categoryId to queryKey array — ensures cache is separate per category
Test: filter by category — confirm only matching products appear
Handle API errors gracefully — catch Axios errors, extract error.response.data.message
Show user-friendly error messages (not raw JSON) when API calls fail
Confirm skeleton loaders still show correctly while real API fetches
Test with Railway backend offline — confirm error UI shows, not a blank screen
Add retry logic: useQuery retries 2 times on failure (TanStack Query default is 3 — reduce to 2)

weeks.askPrompt: Show me the complete Axios instance setup for a React app — with baseURL from env, a token store module, request interceptor that attaches JWT, and a response interceptor that handles 401 logout

weeks.title: Week 2 — Full JWT auth flow end-to-end
weeks.description: Register, login, token storage, protected routes — all wired to the real Spring Boot auth API.

days.label: Day 1 · Monday
days.title: Register flow — React to Spring Boot

tasks.text: Update useRegister() mutation: POST /api/v1/auth/register with {name, email, password}
On success: show success toast, navigate to /login automatically
On error: catch 409 Conflict (email already exists) — show "Email already registered" message
On error: catch 400 validation errors from Spring — display field-level messages
Test full flow: register in React → check MySQL Workbench → confirm user row with hashed password

days.label: Day 2 · Tuesday
days.title: Login flow + token storage

tasks.text: Update useLogin() mutation: POST /api/v1/auth/login with {email, password}
On success: extract token and user from response body
Call AuthContext login() — store token in memory, write user info to localStorage
Write token to the token store module (so Axios interceptor can read it)
Navigate to / (or the page they were trying to reach before being redirected)
On error: show "Invalid email or password" — do not reveal which one is wrong

days.resource: Security rule: Never say "email not found" or "wrong password" separately — that lets attackers enumerate valid accounts. Always say "Invalid credentials" for both cases.

days.label: Day 3 · Wednesday
days.title: Session persistence on page refresh

tasks.text: Problem: on page refresh, React state is lost — user appears logged out even with valid session
In AuthContext: on mount (useEffect []), read user from localStorage
Call GET /api/v1/auth/me with stored token — verify it's still valid on the server
If valid: restore user to state and token to token store — silent re-login
If invalid/expired: clear localStorage, stay logged out
Add a GET /api/v1/auth/me endpoint in Spring Boot (returns current user from JWT)
Test: login, refresh page — confirm user stays logged in

days.resource: Add to Spring Boot: @GetMapping("/me") with @AuthenticationPrincipal UserDetails user — returns user details extracted from the JWT by your filter.

tasks.text: Confirm ProtectedRoute redirects to /login for unauthenticated users
Store the originally requested URL: redirect to it after successful login
Build AdminRoute: extends ProtectedRoute — also checks user.role === 'ADMIN'
Show "Access denied" page if logged in but not an admin (403 scenario)
In Navbar: show "Admin" link only if user.role === 'ADMIN'
Test: login as USER — confirm /admin redirects. Login as ADMIN — confirm access granted
Logout button: call AuthContext logout() — clear state, localStorage, token store
After logout: navigate to /login, invalidate all TanStack Query caches (queryClient.clear())
Token expiry: response interceptor catches 401 → auto-logout + redirect to /login
Show "Your session expired, please log in again" toast on auto-logout
Test: manually expire JWT (shorten expiry in Spring Boot to 10s), wait, make API call — confirm auto-logout

weeks.askPrompt: Show me how to implement session persistence on page refresh in React — reading from localStorage, calling a /auth/me endpoint to verify the JWT is still valid, then restoring the auth state

weeks.title: Week 3 — Authenticated mutations + file uploads
weeks.description: Wire admin features, image uploads, and user-specific data to your real backend.

days.label: Day 1 · Monday
days.title: Admin product CRUD wired to API

tasks.text: Wire useCreateProduct(): POST /api/v1/products with JWT — admin only
Wire useUpdateProduct(id): PUT /api/v1/products/{id} with JWT
Wire useDeleteProduct(id): DELETE /api/v1/products/{id} with JWT
After create/update/delete: invalidate ['products'] queries so list refreshes
Build admin product table: list all products with Edit and Delete buttons per row
Test: create a product as admin in React → verify it appears in MySQL and on product list

days.label: Day 2 · Tuesday
days.title: Image upload — React to Spring Boot

tasks.text: Build image upload input in the admin product form — file input, accept="image/*"
On file select: show a local preview using URL.createObjectURL(file)
On form submit: construct FormData, append image file and product fields
POST FormData to /api/v1/products/{id}/image with Content-Type: multipart/form-data
Display returned imageUrl on the ProductCard and ProductDetailPage
Test: upload a product image — confirm file saved on server, URL returned, image shows in UI

days.resource: Axios tip: When sending FormData, do NOT manually set Content-Type header — let Axios set it automatically with the correct boundary string. Setting it manually breaks multipart parsing.

tasks.text: Build Order entity in Spring Boot: id, user (FK), status, totalAmount, createdAt
Build OrderItem entity: id, order (FK), product (FK), quantity, price
Build POST /api/v1/orders — creates order from cart items (authenticated)
Build GET /api/v1/orders/my — returns orders for the currently logged-in user only
React: build OrderHistoryPage — calls useMyOrders() hook, lists orders with status
React: build "Place Order" button on CartPage — posts cart as an order, clears cart on success
Spring Boot: GET /api/v1/orders — all orders, admin only, paginated
Spring Boot: PATCH /api/v1/orders/{id}/status — update order status, admin only
React: build AdminOrdersPage — table of all orders with status dropdown per row
On status change: PATCH endpoint, invalidate ['orders'] query on success
Test full order flow: add to cart → place order → admin changes status → user sees update

days.label: Day 5 · Friday
days.title: Optimistic updates + query invalidation patterns

tasks.text: Learn: optimistic update — update UI immediately before server confirms, roll back on error
Apply to delete product: remove from UI instantly, restore if DELETE fails
Apply to order status change: show new status immediately in admin table
Learn: onMutate, onError, onSettled callbacks in useMutation for optimistic pattern
Review all mutations — ensure every one invalidates the right query keys on success

days.resource: Resource: tanstack.com/query/latest/docs/framework/react/guides/optimistic-updates — official guide with complete code example

weeks.askPrompt: Show me how to upload an image file from a React form to a Spring Boot multipart endpoint using Axios and FormData — including the local preview and the backend controller code

weeks.title: Week 4 — Production deployment + end-to-end testing
weeks.description: Deploy both apps, connect them live, and verify the entire system works in production.

days.label: Day 1 · Monday
days.title: Harden Spring Boot for production

tasks.text: Move all secrets to Railway environment variables: DB_URL, DB_USER, DB_PASS, JWT_SECRET, JWT_EXPIRY
Set spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto=validate (not update) in production profile
Set CORS allowedOrigins to exact Vercel production URL only (no wildcard *)
Disable Swagger UI in production: springdoc.swagger-ui.enabled=false
Set logging.level.root=WARN in production — reduce log noise
Push to main branch — confirm Railway auto-deploys and app starts cleanly

days.resource: Spring profiles: Create application-prod.properties with production overrides. Set SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE=prod in Railway environment variables to activate it.

days.label: Day 2 · Tuesday
days.title: Deploy React to Vercel — production config

tasks.text: In Vercel dashboard: set VITE_API_URL to your Railway production backend URL
Confirm vercel.json has the SPA rewrite rule — all paths route to index.html
Set up a Vercel preview environment: VITE_API_URL pointing to a dev Railway service
Push a change — confirm Vercel builds and deploys automatically from main
Run Lighthouse audit on the live Vercel URL — note Performance, Accessibility scores
Test on a real mobile device — not just DevTools emulation
Test USER flow: register → verify email (if implemented) → login → browse products → add to cart → place order → view order history
Test ADMIN flow: login as admin → create product with image → edit product → change order status
Test session: login → close tab → reopen → confirm still logged in
Test edge cases: checkout with empty cart, access /admin as USER, register with existing email
Test on multiple browsers: Chrome, Firefox, Safari (mobile) — fix any inconsistencies
Open DevTools → Network tab — look for unnecessary repeated API calls
Check TanStack Query devtools — are staleTime values set on frequently-read queries?
Check image sizes — compress product images, use correct srcset if large
Verify Spring Boot response times in Railway logs — any slow queries?
Add MySQL index on commonly filtered columns (e.g. products.category_id, products.name)
Run npm run build locally — confirm bundle size is reasonable (aim under 500kb gzipped)
Backend README: architecture diagram, API endpoint table, env vars, local setup steps
Frontend README: tech stack, features list, screenshots, live URL, local setup steps
Take 4-5 screenshots of the live app — product listing, product detail, cart, admin panel
Add screenshots to both READMEs — makes GitHub repos look professional
Pin both repos on your GitHub profile
Write a short LinkedIn post describing what you built — name the tech stack and link the live app

weeks.tip: Write down every bug you find on paper. Fix them one by one — don't try to fix multiple things simultaneously or you won't know what fixed what.
weeks.askPrompt: How do I configure Spring Boot application-prod.properties for Railway deployment — with environment variable placeholders for DB credentials, JWT secret, and production-safe JPA settings?

weeks.title: Phase 4 project — live full-stack e-commerce app
weeks.description: The complete system: React on Vercel talking to Spring Boot on Railway over a real MySQL database.

projectCards.heading: What you'll have built
projectCards.text: A deployed, production-grade e-commerce application — users can browse, search, filter, add to cart, register, log in, and place real orders. Admins can manage products (with image upload), categories, and order statuses through a protected dashboard.

projectCards.heading: Architecture
projectCards.text: React (Vercel) → HTTPS → Spring Boot (Railway) → MySQL (Railway) · JWT auth flows through Axios interceptors · Image files stored on Railway filesystem (or Cloudflare R2) · Environment variables manage all credentials

tasks.text: Register and login work end-to-end with real Spring Boot JWT
Session persists across page refresh via /auth/me verification
Expired token auto-logs user out with toast notification
Products load from MySQL with real pagination and search
Admin can create/edit/delete products with image upload
Cart persists in React context, checkout creates real order in DB
User can view their own order history
Admin can view all orders and update statuses
CORS locked to Vercel origin only in production
No secrets in Git — all credentials in environment variables
Both repos have screenshots and setup docs in README
Live URL tested on mobile, Chrome, Firefox, Safari

weeks.askPrompt: Help me debug a CORS issue between my React app on Vercel and my Spring Boot API on Railway — what are the most common causes and how do I fix each one?
